plus.analyst_notes:
Closes https://github.com/vz-risk/VCDB/issues/23572
Encoded by AI — encode-veris-incident skill version 20260713T202114Z

Sources consulted: BankInfoSecurity (https://www.bankinfosecurity.com/hipaas-no-joke-gag-gift-firms-health-plan-pays-450k-fine-a-32032); HIPAA Journal (https://www.hipaajournal.com/spencer-gifts-hipaa-settlement/); HHS OCR press release (https://www.hhs.gov/press-room/ocr-settles-ransomware-investigation-health-plan.html).

Victim is the employer-sponsored health plan (Spencer Gifts LLC Flexible Benefits and Welfare Benefit Plans), not Spencer Gifts LLC retail. NAICS 524 follows pipeline label.

Timeline: Conti accessed the network between November 24-26, 2021. Discovery was when employees reported inability to connect to VPN (November 2021). Breach notified to OCR on January 24, 2022. HHS OCR announced $450,000 HIPAA settlement and corrective action plan in 2026.

Actor: Conti ransomware group claimed responsibility on their dark website in January 2022 (per BankInfoSecurity). Initial access vector not disclosed by sources; coded as Unknown. Conti is an organized cybercrime group known for double extortion. Data exfiltration coded as Exfiltrate in malware result but is attacker-claimed and not victim-confirmed; sources describe data as 'potentially stolen.' Noted in event_chain.

Data: PHI of 10,023 health plan members exposed including names, addresses, zip codes, phone numbers, email addresses, and Social Security numbers. Medical coded for PHI context; Personal for names/contact info; Sensitive Personal for SSNs. data_total set to 10,023 (confirmed count from settlement).

Employee count: Spencer's Gifts employs approximately 10,000-15,000 people per business directory sources; coded 10001 to 25000.

DBIR year 2023 derived from November 24, 2021 incident begin date (November is past Oct 31 cutoff, so Y+2 = 2023).

PHIDBR2019 label ignored per VCDB policy — artifact tag only, year in label is not incident year.

event_chain.summary: Threat actor gains initial access to Spencer Gifts network by unknown method
Conti ransomware installed on company servers including those storing health plan ePHI
Health plan member PHI potentially exfiltrated prior to encryption (Conti double-extortion MO; not victim-confirmed)
Files encrypted, preventing employees from connecting to VPN and accessing systems

summary: In November 2021, the Conti ransomware group accessed Spencer Gifts LLC's network and deployed ransomware, encrypting files on servers that stored health plan members' ePHI. Employees discovered the breach when unable to connect to the VPN. Conti claimed responsibility on their dark website in January 2022. The incident exposed names, addresses, phone numbers, email addresses, and Social Security numbers of 10,023 health plan members. The employer-sponsored health plan paid a $450,000 HIPAA settlement and agreed to a corrective action plan in 2026.

victim.victim_id: Spencer Gifts LLC Flexible Benefits and Welfare Benefit Plans
